artifact: John the Dwarf 4 — Thought Already Begun
original: John the Dwarf warns that excess and careless intimacy begin the fall in thought.
english: The act is late; the thought has already opened the door.
spanish: El acto llega tarde; el pensamiento ya abrió la puerta.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 4
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 4, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

artifact: John the Dwarf 5 — Flee the Anger
original: Seeing anger arise on the road, John leaves his goods and flees.
english: When anger takes the road, leave the baggage and save the heart.
spanish: Cuando la ira toma el camino, deja la carga y salva el corazón.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 5
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 5, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 6 — Leave the Harvest
original: Hearing angry speech in summer, John leaves the harvest and flees.
english: No harvest is worth gathering if it gathers anger with it.
spanish: Ninguna cosecha vale la pena si junto a ella se cosecha ira.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 6
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 6, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 7 — Accept the Cup
original: John accepts service from the priest so the priest is not deprived of his reward.
english: Humility can receive, so another may not be wounded by refusal.
spanish: La humildad también sabe recibir, para no herir al otro con el rechazo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 7
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 7, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 8 — Vessel of Poison
original: When accused of poison, John says the outside alone was seen.
english: The humble answer: you saw only the outside, and still you saw enough.
spanish: La respuesta humilde: viste sólo lo de afuera, y aun así viste bastante.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 8
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 8, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 9 — Tears at the Meal
original: At the common meal, John weeps when a brother laughs.
english: Even joy must know where it is sitting and what it has been given.
spanish: Hasta la alegría debe saber dónde se sienta y qué ha recibido.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 9
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 9, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 10 — Rain in the Heart
original: As rain renews palms, the Spirit renews hearts and brings forth leaves.
english: When the heart receives rain, fear of God becomes new leaves.
spanish: Cuando el corazón recibe lluvia, el temor de Dios brota como hojas nuevas.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 10
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 10, p. 74. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 11 — Rope and Contemplation
original: John weaves rope without noticing the measure because his spirit is occupied in contemplation.
english: Attention can become so single that even the hands forget their count.
spanish: La atención puede hacerse tan una que hasta las manos olvidan contar.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 11
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 11, p. 75. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 12 — Tree of Prayer
original: John compares prayer to climbing a tree when beasts and snakes approach.
english: When thoughts become beasts, prayer is the tree that still stands.
spanish: Cuando los pensamientos se vuelven bestias, la oración es el árbol que aún queda en pie.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 12
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 12, p. 75. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: John the Dwarf 13 — Strength for the Fight
original: John learns not to ask for warfare to vanish, but for strength inside it.
english: Do not ask first for the battle to end; ask for strength not to abandon it.
spanish: No pidas primero que termine la batalla; pide fuerza para no abandonarla.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 13
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 13, p. 75. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

original: The later monk crosses the sea with weak wings, sometimes under water, sometimes above it.
english: Not every generation is given wings of fire; some cross by weak wings and tears.
spanish: No toda generación recibe alas de fuego; algunas cruzan con alas débiles y lágrimas.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 14
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 14, p. 75. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.
